entries.specific_competence: (b) Describe the concept of electronic arrangements
teaching_learning_activities: Technological-based learning: Guide students to watch visual aids for simulations and animations, showing the arrangements of electrons in atoms of elements in their shells and write a report
Group activity: Assign students in manageable groups to practice the arrangement of electrons in shells of atoms of different elements through models designing using locally available materials
entries.assessment: The concept of electronic arrangements in atoms is clearly described
resources: Diagrams, pictures, wall charts, simulations and videos illustrating energy shell diagram, and locally available materials for designing electronic models
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: (d) Explain the concept of chemical formulae and nomenclature (valence, oxidation state, radicals and naming of binary inorganic compounds using the IUP AC system)
teaching_learning_activities: Inquiry-based learning: Assign students to collect commonly used substances and give their common names and identify their constituent elements
Group discussion: Guide student in manageable groups to describe the concepts of chemical formula, valence, oxidation state, radicals and name binary inorganic compounds using the IUPAC system
Gallery work: Guide students to brainstorm and share key rules in writing chemical formulae
entries.assessment: The concept of chemical formulae and nomenclature are clearly explained
resources: Valence cards, modern periodic table, wall charts showing oxidation states and common radicals, and labelled bottles containing different laboratory chemicals
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: (e) Determine empirical and molecular formulae of common compounds
teaching_learning_activities: Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to determine percentage compositions of each element and in a compound
Problem solving: Guide students to apply steps for calculating empirical formulae and molecular formulae of different compounds
entries.assessment: Empirical formulae and molecular formulae of different compounds are correctly determined
resources: Model depicting empirical formulae and molecular formulae, wall charts showing molecular fomulae and modern periodic table
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: (g) Describe the concept of chemical reactions (chemical equations, balancing chemical equations, and types of chemical reactions)
teaching_learning_activities: Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to write simple word equations and chemical equations with symbols
Think-ink-pair-share: Guide students in pairs to identify types of chemical reactions, balance chemical equations, interpret word equations into chemical equations
Experimentation: Guide students to perform an experiment to verify the law of conservation of matter and write the report
entries.assessment: The concept of chemical reactions is clearly described
resources: Kerosene, candles, KOH/NaOH, HCl, glycerine, selected apparatus and equipment
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: (h) Relate the types of chemical reactions with common processes in daily life such as burning of fuel and digestion in living organisms
teaching_learning_activities: Project works: Task students to carry-out project to examine applications of chemical reactions in the burning of fuel and digestion in living organisms
entries.assessment: The types of chemical reactions are clearly related with common processes in daily life
resources: Laboratory note book, heat source and fuel such as charcoal
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 1.2 Demonstrate an understanding of the physical and chemical properties of elements on the basis of their arrangements in the periodic table (a) Explain the changes in the physical and chemical properties of elements across and down the periodic table
teaching_learning_activities: Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to explain the trends of metallic and non-metallic elements across the periods and down the groups of the periodic table
entries.assessment: Changes in properties of elements across and down the periodic table are correctly explained
resources: Diagrams, pictures, wall charts, simulations and videos illustrating changes in the physical and chemical properties of elements across and down the periodic table
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 1.2 Demonstrate an understanding of the physical and chemical properties of elements on the basis of their arrangements in the periodic table (a) Explain the changes in the physical and chemical properties of elements across and down the periodic table
teaching_learning_activities: Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to explain the trends of metallic and non-metallic elements across the periods and down the groups of the periodic table
entries.assessment: Changes in properties of elements across and down the periodic table are correctly explained
resources: Diagrams, pictures, wall charts, simulations and videos illustrating changes in the physical and chemical properties of elements across and down the periodic table
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: (b) Use the electronic configuration of an element to locate their positions in the periodic table
teaching_learning_activities: Group discusion: Guide students in manageable groups to apply the periodic law in locating elements in the periodic table
entries.assessment: The electronic configurations of elements are correctly used to locate the positions of elements in the periodic table
resources: Wall charts of modern periodic table showing electronic configuration of some elements
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Conduct experiments in Chemistry (a) Prepare acid- base indicators using locally available materials
teaching_learning_activities: Outdoor activity: Guide students to prepare acid- base indicators from locally available materials and write reports
entries.assessment: Acid-base indicators are correctly prepared using locally available materials
resources: Coloured flowers, coloured leaves, mortar and pestle, pieces of clothes, heat source, tripod stand lemon juice, tomato juice, sour milk, vinegar, soft drinks and NaOH
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: (b) Use the prepared indicators to test acidity and alkalinity of substances found in the locality
teaching_learning_activities: Experimentation: Guide students to perform experiments using locally made indicators to test acidity and alkalinity of subtances found in the locality
entries.assessment: Acidity and alkalinity of substances found in the locality are tested using the prepared indicators
resources: Locally prepared indicator, POP indicator, MO indicator, lemon juice, tomato juice, sour milk, vinegar and soft
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts, theories and principles in Chemistry
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of concepts, theories and principles in Chemistry (a) Describe the mole concept (comparison of the mole with other units of measurements and molar quantities of substances)
teaching_learning_activities: Outdoor learning: Assign students to visit nearby shops to explore items that are sold in units such as water in catorns, pair of shoes and other quantities and describe the concept of mole and molar quantities of substances
Classroom assignments: Assign students to work on calculations and conversions of masses and volumes of substances to moles and vice versa
entries.assessment: The mole concept is clearly decribed
resources: Analytical balance, different chemicals, and videos, animations, simulations, pictures/ wall, charts illustrating the molar mass of elements
entries.references: Chemistry Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)
